emails.subject: MDEA
emails.body: Hi Ed, Here's the language they offered: M. Each of the Cities has obligated itself to be responsible for and to pay= =20 to, or on behalf of, MDEA, if required, that proportion of the minimum=20 monthly fee which that City=01,s hourly demand was coincident with Entergy= =01,s=20 peak hour demand in the year 2000 bears to the sum of both Cities=01, hourl= y=20 demand during such hour; provided however, that each City has further=20 obligated itself to be responsible for and to pay that portion of EMPI=01,s= =20 monthly fee which the economic benefit to that City from transactions bears= =20 to the total economic benefit to both Cities from such transactions during= =20 the month. It is a bit cumbersome, but I think I like it. What do you think? I'll forward the entire doc soon. Kay

emails.subject: MDEA
emails.body: MDEA In speaking with Marvin regarding Clarksdale's authority to raise rates, he pointed to me their statutes. Is this something that you have a copy of since this is a public filing. Also, the same for Yazoo. Ed Sacks x5-7712

emails.subject: Re: MDEA
emails.body: Re: MDEA Don't have it, but we can ask local counsel. If you can email me the question you would like asked, I will pass it along to our Miss lawyer. Kay From: Edward Sacks/ENRON@enronXgate on 05/01/2001 09:37 AM

emails.subject: RE: MDEA
emails.body: RE: MDEA In speaking to Marvin, he indicated that Clarksdale's authority and the protocals for increasing rates (electric, water sewer) were detailed in the statute. He indicated that it may be in Section 21, but perhaps Miss legal would know better. Under the statute, he indicated that rate approval is subject to Board Approval. I just want to confirm this info as well as review the same information for Yazoo. I don't need or want a copy of the entire statute, but only the 5-10 pages which might be applicible to the subject above. The same is needed for Yazoo. If this is not specific enough please let me know. thks Ed Sacks

emails.subject: RE: MDEA
emails.body: RE: MDEA In speaking to Marvin, he indicated that Clarksdale's authority and the protocals for increasing rates (electric, water sewer) were detailed in the statute. He indicated that it may be in Section 21, but perhaps Miss legal would know better. Under the statute, he indicated that rate approval is subject to Board Approval. I just want to confirm this info as well as review the same information for Yazoo. I don't need or want a copy of the entire statute, but only the 5-10 pages which might be applicible to the subject above. The same is needed for Yazoo. If this is not specific enough please let me know. thks Ed Sacks

emails.subject: RE: MDEA
emails.body: RE: MDEA What is required for Clarksdale and Yazoo to increase its electric utility rates?
